maintainer_org: FoundationAgents (org rebrand from original maintainer geekan; same canonical repo, redirected). Commercial arm DeepWisdom (deepwisdom.ai) operates the MGX product (rebranded to Atoms 2026-01-13).
execution_kernel_pattern: Role-based assembly-line pipeline driven by Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). SOPs are encoded as Python class hierarchies + Pydantic-typed artifact schemas + prompt templates — NOT YAML. The base `Role` is a Pydantic BaseModel with attributes (name, profile, goal, constraints, actions, states) and a `RoleContext` runtime state (msg_buffer, memory, state index, todo, watch). Each role subclass (ProductManager, Architect, ProjectManager, Engineer, QAEngineer, Researcher, DataInterpreter) declares its `_actions` and `_watch` (message types it subscribes to). Actions subclass `Action` and emit typed artifacts via `ActionNode` (Pydantic models like WRITE_PRD_NODE serialized to JSON). Execution flows through three reaction modes: REACT (LLM picks next state via STATE_TEMPLATE prompt), BY_ORDER (fixed sequence), PLAN_AND_ACT (planner generates task DAG, then executes). Inter-role communication uses an Environment message bus: roles publish typed Messages, downstream roles filter by `rc.watch` set and react when their input artifact appears.

multi_agent_support.style: role-play / software-company simulation with rigid SOP-driven workflow
multi_agent_support.notes: Mechanically distinct from CrewAI (flat role+task DSL, sequential or hierarchical process), AutoGen (generic conversation graph between ConversableAgents), and ChatDev (Chat-Chain of two-agent atomic chats with inception prompting). MetaGPT's distinctive primitive: typed artifact handoffs over a publish/subscribe message bus, with `_watch` declaratively binding consumer roles to producer action types. Recent additions: Data Interpreter (autonomous data-science role with PLAN_AND_ACT mode), MGX/Atoms product line, MCP Registry integration.

tool_protocol: Native Python Action classes per role; MCP Registry integration added in 2025 (still a shim layer, not the dominant tool pathway). No first-class function-calling abstraction comparable to OpenAI Agents SDK; tools wired per-role through Action subclasses.
memory_model: Per-role short-term message memory (RoleContext.memory) + shared Environment memory (artifacts published as Messages). Long-term memory via vector store optional. Artifact-passing (Pydantic-typed Documents serialized to JSON/markdown on disk) dominates over free-text scratchpad — code base writes PRD.md, design.md, task_list.md etc. as durable on-disk state.
observability_story: Logs to file + console; on-disk Pydantic-serialized artifacts (PRD.md, design.md, task_list.md, code files, test reports) serve as durable audit trail. No first-class OpenTelemetry tracing; users wire their own.
typical_use_case: Generate a small-to-medium software project end-to-end from a one-line requirement (2048 game, CLI blackjack, snake); academic baseline for multi-agent code generation; Data Interpreter for autonomous data analysis pipelines; commercial productization via MGX/Atoms (full-stack app generation as a SaaS).
maturity_signal: Mature and actively maintained: 68.3k stars, MIT, commits through 2026-01-21 (Windows terminal adaptation), but cadence slowed to ~monthly merges in 2025 vs near-daily 2023-24. Org rebranded geekan -> FoundationAgents reflecting expanded scope (MGX/Atoms commercial product, AFlow/SPO/AOT research papers). Strong academic credentials: ICLR 2024 oral (MetaGPT) + ICLR 2025 oral (AFlow). Commercial: MGX hit 500k registered users and $1M ARR within one month of Feb 2025 launch (zero ad spend); rebranded to Atoms 2026-01-13 with backing from Ant Group, Cathay Capital, Jinqiu. NOT abandoned — rebrand is upward (research lab + commercial venture), not orphaning.
one_paragraph_differentiator: MetaGPT is the only major OSS multi-agent framework that treats Standard Operating Procedures as a first-class orchestration primitive baked into Python class hierarchies + Pydantic artifact schemas + a typed message bus, rather than a pattern users assemble themselves. Where CrewAI gives a thin role+task DSL and AutoGen a generic conversation graph, MetaGPT ships an opinionated software-company SOP (PRD -> architecture -> task plan -> code -> review) wired through declarative `_watch` subscriptions and `ActionNode` Pydantic outputs. It is closest in spirit to ChatDev — both academic, both virtual software companies — but mechanically different: ChatDev composes a Chat-Chain of two-agent atomic chats (instructor + assistant with inception prompting and communicative dehallucination, role-flip mitigation), while MetaGPT runs N specialized roles in parallel over a typed pub/sub bus with on-disk Pydantic artifacts as the durable medium. The MetaGPT team has also pushed the frontier toward self-discovered workflows via AFlow (ICLR 2025 oral, arXiv 2410.10762), which uses MCTS over code-represented workflow graphs with reusable Operators (Generate, Format, Review, Revise, Ensemble, Test, Programmer) to automatically synthesize workflows that beat hand-coded baselines by 5.7% on six benchmarks (HumanEval, MBPP, GSM8K, MATH, HotpotQA, DROP) — signaling the framework's research trajectory is toward AutoML-of-agents.
known_anti_patterns: Forcing non-software-engineering tasks through the PRD->design->code SOP wastes tokens and produces awkward artifacts
SOP rigidity means failure modes in one stage (e.g., bad PRD) cascade rather than self-correct, despite intermediate verification via ActionNode schemas
Heavy Python subclassing required for per-role customization (subclass Role + override _actions/_watch + new Action + new ActionNode) — invasive compared to CrewAI's data-class DSL
Benchmarks emphasize toy software projects (2048, snake, blackjack) — production-scale codebase generation is not demonstrated
MCP integration is announced but shim-level; not the dominant tool pathway as of 2026-01
analysis_depth: deep — source-verified: read metagpt/roles/role.py (Pydantic Role + RoleContext + reaction modes), metagpt/actions/write_prd.py (ActionNode + Pydantic artifact serialization), AFlow arXiv 2410.10762 + repo (MCTS over code-represented workflows with Operators), MGX/Atoms product trajectory, and ChatDev arXiv 2307.07924 mechanical comparison (Chat-Chain + atomic chats + instructor/assistant inception prompting). All 4 explicit gaps closed.
